body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**`--output` is effectively a no-op right now.**

`runAdminReadStream` reads `--output`, but `renderFrame` ignores it (`_ string`) and always emits text. `--output json` currently cannot work.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 import (
 	"encoding/hex"
 	"fmt"
 	"io"
 	"strings"
 	"time"

 	"connectrpc.com/connect"
 	"github.com/samber/oops"
 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+	"google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protojson"
 	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
@@
 	output, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output") //nolint:errcheck // flag defined in newAdminReadStreamCmd
+	if output != "text" && output != "json" {
+		return &exitCodeError{
+			exitCode: 64, // EX_USAGE
+			cause:    oops.Code("ADMIN_READSTREAM_BAD_OUTPUT").Errorf("unsupported output format %q (want text|json)", output),
+		}
+	}
@@
-func renderFrame(frame *adminv1.AdminReadStreamResponse, _ string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) {
+func renderFrame(frame *adminv1.AdminReadStreamResponse, output string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) {
+	if output == "json" {
+		b, err := protojson.Marshal(frame)
+		if err == nil {
+			fmt.Fprintln(stdout, string(b)) //nolint:errcheck // non-fatal CLI output
+		}
+		return
+	}
 	switch {
```
</details>


Also applies to: 71-71, 248-289

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@cmd/holomush/cmd_admin_read_stream.go` around lines 44 - 45, The --output
flag is read in runAdminReadStream but ignored because renderFrame currently
takes a placeholder (_ string) and always emits text; update runAdminReadStream
to parse the "output" flag and pass it into renderFrame (replace the "_"
parameter) and modify renderFrame to branch on the format ("text" vs "json") and
emit valid JSON when "json" is requested (serialize the frame structure or
selected fields). Also update any other renderFrame call sites noted (around the
other locations) to supply the chosen format or default to "text" so --output
actually controls output.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Fix accidental list marker in paragraph flow.**

Line 113/Line 114 currently split one sentence into a dangling bullet, which makes the rationale section read like malformed list content.

<details>
<summary>📝 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-F has no plugin identity (operator capability is the principal), checks
-capability once at handler entry (never per-row), and emits one pre-data
-
-- one post-data audit pair (not per-decrypt). Passing null-objects for
+F has no plugin identity (operator capability is the principal), checks
+capability once at handler entry (never per-row), and emits one pre-data
+plus one post-data audit pair (not per-decrypt). Passing null-objects for
 fields that don't apply is a code smell pointing at a bundle that should
 not include F.
```
</details>

 

As per coding guidelines, docs lists must use consistent list markers and proper structure.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/adr/0017-admin-readstream-bypasses-history-reader.md` around lines 113 -
115, The paragraph has an accidental list marker splitting the sentence; remove
the leading "-" before the fragment "one post-data audit pair (not
per-decrypt)." and merge that fragment back into the surrounding sentence so the
rationale reads as a single continuous paragraph (ensure the phrase "one
post-data audit pair (not per-decrypt)" appears inline rather than as a bullet).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- d98c2f50 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Invariant-count statement conflicts with the catalog below.**

Line 112 says `INV-F4`, `INV-F5`, and `INV-F18` are deleted (13 remain), but Section 6/Coverage matrix still enumerates those IDs. Please align the declaration and catalog so implementers/test authors get one consistent target.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In
`@docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-11-event-payload-crypto-phase5-sub-epic-f-design.md`
around lines 112 - 113, Summary: The invariants count declaration (Line 112)
conflicts with Section 6's catalog which still lists INV-F4, INV-F5, and
INV-F18; align them so the document is consistent. Fix: decide whether INV-F4,
INV-F5, and INV-F18 are deleted or retained, then update both the summary line
that states "INV-F4, INV-F5, INV-F18 deleted (13 remain)" and the Section 6 /
Coverage matrix entries to match that decision; specifically, if deleting those
invariants remove their rows/IDs from the catalog and adjust the remaining
count, otherwise restore them in the summary and update the count to include
them. Ensure all references to INV-F4, INV-F5, and INV-F18 elsewhere in the doc
(e.g., cross-references, index) are updated to the chosen state.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Enforce non-empty `excludePlayerID` to preserve dual-control guarantees.**

Line 131 currently accepts empty `excludePlayerID`; in that case, `primary_player_id != $3` won’t prevent selecting approvals authored by the same operator. This should fail closed.

<details>
<summary>🔒 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 import (
 	"context"
 	"crypto/rand"
 	"errors"
+	"strings"
 	"time"
@@
 func (r *PostgresRepo) GetByOpArgsHash(ctx context.Context, opKind string, opArgsHash []byte, excludePlayerID string) (Approval, error) {
+	if strings.TrimSpace(excludePlayerID) == "" {
+		return Approval{}, oops.Code("APPROVAL_INVALID_ARGUMENT").
+			Errorf("exclude_player_id is required")
+	}
+
 	row := r.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/approval/repo.go` around lines 131 - 145, GetByOpArgsHash
currently allows an empty excludePlayerID which makes the SQL condition
primary_player_id != $3 ineffective; change GetByOpArgsHash to fail closed by
validating the excludePlayerID parameter at the start of the function (in the
method GetByOpArgsHash) and return a clear error if it's empty, so the SQL query
never runs with an empty exclusion value; update any callers/tests to ensure
they pass a non-empty excludePlayerID or handle the returned error.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Harden `ScopeFromSubject` against malformed scope suffixes.**

Line 61 currently accepts empty or multi-segment suffixes after the prefix. Reject invalid suffixes so only a single non-empty request scope is accepted.

 
<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 		ScopeFromSubject: func(subject string) (string, error) {
 			if !strings.HasPrefix(subject, prefixDot) {
 				return "", oops.Code("OPERATOR_READ_SCOPE_FROM_SUBJECT_FAILED").
 					With("subject", subject).
 					With("expected_prefix", prefixDot).
 					Errorf("subject prefix mismatch")
 			}
-			return subject[len(prefixDot):], nil
+			scope := subject[len(prefixDot):]
+			if scope == "" || strings.Contains(scope, ".") {
+				return "", oops.Code("OPERATOR_READ_SCOPE_FROM_SUBJECT_FAILED").
+					With("subject", subject).
+					With("expected_format", prefixDot+"<request_id>").
+					Errorf("invalid scope segment")
+			}
+			return scope, nil
 		},
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
		ScopeFromSubject: func(subject string) (string, error) {
			if !strings.HasPrefix(subject, prefixDot) {
				return "", oops.Code("OPERATOR_READ_SCOPE_FROM_SUBJECT_FAILED").
					With("subject", subject).
					With("expected_prefix", prefixDot).
					Errorf("subject prefix mismatch")
			}
			scope := subject[len(prefixDot):]
			if scope == "" || strings.Contains(scope, ".") {
				return "", oops.Code("OPERATOR_READ_SCOPE_FROM_SUBJECT_FAILED").
					With("subject", subject).
					With("expected_format", prefixDot+"<request_id>").
					Errorf("invalid scope segment")
			}
			return scope, nil
		},
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/readstream/chain.go` around lines 54 - 62, ScopeFromSubject
currently returns any suffix after prefixDot, including empty or multi-segment
values; update ScopeFromSubject (the ScopeFromSubject function and its return
path using prefixDot) to validate the extracted suffix: after confirming
strings.HasPrefix(subject, prefixDot) compute suffix :=
subject[len(prefixDot):], then if suffix == "" or strings.Contains(suffix, ".")
return an oops.Code("OPERATOR_READ_SCOPE_FROM_SUBJECT_FAILED") with subject and
expected_prefix and a clear message (e.g., "invalid or multi-segment scope")
instead of returning the suffix; otherwise return the suffix and nil.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Timeout path can block indefinitely after deadline fires.**

At **Line 61**, waiting on `doneCh` before returning means a stuck `send(frame)` can prevent `ErrWriteDeadlineExceeded` from ever being returned, defeating the per-frame deadline behavior.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 	case <-timerCtx.Done():
 		// Either the per-frame deadline fired, or the parent ctx was cancelled.
@@
-		// Drain the orphaned goroutine before returning to prevent data races.
-		<-doneCh
-
 		return returnErr
 	}
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/readstream/deadline_writer.go` around lines 47 - 63, The
current timeout path blocks on `<-doneCh` which can hang if the send(frame)
goroutine is stuck; change the draining logic in the timerCtx.Done() branch to
avoid blocking: replace the blocking receive on doneCh with a non-blocking drain
(use a select that attempts to read from doneCh but falls through immediately if
it would block) so ErrWriteDeadlineExceeded can be returned promptly; keep the
same variables (timerCtx, ctx, doneCh, ErrWriteDeadlineExceeded) and ensure any
orphaned goroutine is still observed without waiting indefinitely.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add a nil-request guard in `ResolveBounds` to prevent panic.**

Line 91 dereferences `req` unconditionally. A nil input will panic instead of returning a typed validation error.

 
<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 func ResolveBounds(req *Request, now time.Time, defaultWindow, maxWindow time.Duration) (Resolved, ResolvedFlags, error) {
+	if req == nil {
+		return Resolved{}, ResolvedFlags{}, oops.
+			Code("DENY_OPERATOR_READ_INVALID_REQUEST").
+			Errorf("request must not be nil")
+	}
+
 	var flags ResolvedFlags
 
 	// --- Defaulting (applied before temporal validation) ---
 	since := req.Since
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
func ResolveBounds(req *Request, now time.Time, defaultWindow, maxWindow time.Duration) (Resolved, ResolvedFlags, error) {
	if req == nil {
		return Resolved{}, ResolvedFlags{}, oops.
			Code("DENY_OPERATOR_READ_INVALID_REQUEST").
			Errorf("request must not be nil")
	}

	var flags ResolvedFlags

	// --- Defaulting (applied before temporal validation) ---
	since := req.Since
	until := req.Until
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/readstream/filter.go` around lines 87 - 93, ResolveBounds
currently dereferences req (e.g., since := req.Since) without a nil guard which
will panic for a nil Request; add an early check at the top of ResolveBounds: if
req == nil { return Resolved{}, ResolvedFlags{}, validationError } (use the same
typed validation error pattern used elsewhere in the package) so callers receive
a proper error instead of a panic; reference Request, ResolveBounds, and
ResolvedFlags when adding this guard.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Reject `DefaultWindow > MaxWindow` during validation.**

The config currently boots as long as both values are positive. If `DefaultWindow` is larger than `MaxWindow`, every request that relies on defaulted bounds will be rejected later by `ResolveBounds`, which makes the feature unusable until someone notices the runtime failures. Fail fast here instead.

<details>
<summary>Minimal fix</summary>

```diff
 	case c.DefaultWindow <= 0:
 		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("DefaultWindow must be positive")
+	case c.DefaultWindow > c.MaxWindow:
+		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("DefaultWindow must be <= MaxWindow")
 	case c.WriteDeadline <= 0:
 		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("WriteDeadline must be positive")
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
	case c.MaxWindow <= 0:
		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("MaxWindow must be positive")
	case c.DefaultWindow <= 0:
		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("DefaultWindow must be positive")
	case c.DefaultWindow > c.MaxWindow:
		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("DefaultWindow must be <= MaxWindow")
	case c.WriteDeadline <= 0:
		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("WriteDeadline must be positive")
	case c.ApprovalTTL <= 0:
		return oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("ApprovalTTL must be positive")
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/readstream/handler.go` around lines 140 - 147, Add a
validation that rejects configurations where DefaultWindow exceeds MaxWindow: in
the same validation switch/block that checks c.MaxWindow, c.DefaultWindow, etc.
(the case branches currently returning
oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf(...)), add a new case checking if
c.DefaultWindow > c.MaxWindow and return
oops.Code("READSTREAM_CONFIG_INVALID").Errorf("DefaultWindow must not exceed
MaxWindow") so the handler fails fast before ResolveBounds is ever called.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Bind dual-control approval reuse to the effective request.**

Line 237 hashes the raw proto after Line 231 has already resolved/defaulted the actual read window. That means two requests that omit `since`/`until` can hash the same even when they execute against different effective time ranges, so `GetByOpArgsHash` can reuse an approval for data that was never actually approved. The same path also drops `DualControlTimeoutSeconds` from the key and still waits on `h.cfg.ApprovalTTL`, so the request-level timeout never affects reuse or wait behavior.

<details>
<summary>Suggested direction</summary>

```diff
- opArgsHash, err := computeReadStreamArgsHash(req)
+ effectiveTTL := effectiveApprovalTTL(req, h.cfg.ApprovalTTL)
+ opArgsHash, err := computeReadStreamArgsHash(resolved, effectiveTTL)

- approvalInfo, err = h.acquireApproval(ctx, session, opArgsHash, stream)
+ approvalInfo, err = h.acquireApproval(ctx, session, opArgsHash, effectiveTTL, stream)
```

Build the hash from the canonicalized request the handler will actually execute (`resolved.Contexts`, `resolved.Since`, `resolved.Until`, normalized justification, effective TTL), not from the raw wire message.
</details>

 


Also applies to: 242-259, 356-369, 680-697

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/admin/readstream/handler.go` around lines 229 - 240, The op-args
hash is being computed from the raw proto (computeReadStreamArgsHash(req))
before ResolveBounds, causing approvals to be reused across different effective
windows and ignoring request-level DualControlTimeoutSeconds; change the code to
compute the hash from the canonicalized/normalized request that will actually
execute (use the output of protoToDomesticRequest + resolved values returned by
ResolveBounds), including resolved.Contexts, resolved.Since, resolved.Until, the
normalized justification text, and the effective TTL/DualControlTimeoutSeconds
(not h.cfg.ApprovalTTL) in the hash input; update all callsites that use
computeReadStreamArgsHash/GetByOpArgsHash around the same flow (the other noted
ranges) to use this new canonicalized-hash input so approvals and wait behavior
are bound to the executed request.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Stabilize source-file path resolution in the static-analysis test.**

Line 134 says paths are resolved via `runtime.Caller`, but Line 155 still reads raw relative paths. This can break under runners with a different working directory. Resolve absolute paths once from the current test file location.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 import (
 	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"runtime"
 	"testing"
@@
 func TestINV_F16_HotColdStampersDoNotEmitNewValues(t *testing.T) {
-	// Paths are relative to the module root. The test must be run from the
-	// repo root (task test handles this) or with the correct working directory.
-	// We resolve via runtime.Caller to get the module root.
-	files := []string{
-		"../../internal/eventbus/history/cold_postgres.go",
-		"../../internal/eventbus/history/dispatcher.go",
-		"../../internal/eventbus/subscriber.go",
-	}
+	_, thisFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
+	require.True(t, ok, "runtime.Caller must resolve current test file")
+	moduleRoot := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "..", ".."))
+	files := []string{
+		filepath.Join(moduleRoot, "internal/eventbus/history/cold_postgres.go"),
+		filepath.Join(moduleRoot, "internal/eventbus/history/dispatcher.go"),
+		filepath.Join(moduleRoot, "internal/eventbus/subscriber.go"),
+	}
```
</details>


Also applies to: 155-156

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@internal/eventbus/types_proto_sync_test.go` around lines 134 - 141, The test
currently uses raw relative strings in the files slice which can break when the
working directory differs; compute the absolute paths once from the test file
location by calling runtime.Caller(0) to get the current test file path, set
baseDir := filepath.Dir(filename), and replace the raw entries in files with
filepath.Join(baseDir, "<relative/path>") (or map the existing files slice
through filepath.Join(baseDir, p)). Update the code that references the files
slice (the files variable) so it uses these resolved paths; reference
runtime.Caller, filepath.Dir, filepath.Join and the files variable when making
the change.
```

</details>
